observations.statement: Documentation describes GET /api/v2/awards/{award_id}/ as the award-profile endpoint for a specific award and says the path parameter accepts either a generated award hash or an internal database id.
Documentation defines three category branches: ContractResponse, IDVResponse, and FinancialAssistanceResponse, all returned as a single JSON object keyed to one requested award.

The documented response families preserve common award-summary fields plus category-specific sections such as latest_transaction_contract_data, parent_award, naics_hierarchy, and psc_hierarchy for procurement awards and cfda_info and funding_opportunity for assistance awards.

Documentation lists common top-level financial and summary fields including total_obligation, subaward_count, total_subaward_amount, total_account_outlay, total_account_obligation, account_outlays_by_defc, account_obligations_by_defc, awarding_agency, recipient, period_of_performance, place_of_performance, and executive_details.
The financial-assistance data structure spells the field name as funding_opportunty, while the example payload earlier in the same document shows funding_opportunity.

The documented contract and assistance data structures do not describe total_outlay consistently across award families, even though the endpoint documentation examples and current profile mention the field.
The current raw profile exposes a single path request field, award_id, and records prior successful calls for generated hashes, numeric award ids, IDV examples, and assistance examples on this route.

The current raw profile records prior route quirks: requests without the trailing slash returned HTTP 301, lower-case hashes returned an HTML 404 page, invalid numeric ids returned a JSON 404 payload, and assistance request hashes could be normalized before being echoed back in generated_unique_award_id.

The current raw profile records prior observations that assistance payloads can return funding_agency as null, that total_outlay appears in live responses despite uneven documentation, and that generated_internal_id from search/spending_by_award is the safer follow-up identifier than the display Award ID field.

source.title: AwardRetrieveViewSet request parsing and category dispatch

observations.statement: Source code treats a digit-only path value as integer award_id, otherwise validates it as generated_unique_award_id text.
If no AwardSearch row matches generated_unique_award_id, the view retries the lookup using generated_unique_award_id_legacy before raising NotFound.
Response construction branches by award category: contract uses construct_contract_response, idv uses construct_idv_response, and all other categories use construct_assistance_response.
Source response builders add funding_opportunity to assistance responses and total_outlay to assistance, contract, and idv responses.

Source response builders derive total_account_outlay, total_account_obligation, account_outlays_by_defc, and account_obligations_by_defc from FinancialAccountsByAwards / DEFC data, while total_outlay is a separate computed measure based on final-balance outlay columns.
Recipient and place-of-performance objects are assembled as nested location records, and funding_agency / awarding_agency objects are nullable when no Agency lookup exists.

source.title: Derived request coverage check for award detail endpoint

observations.statement: The staged docs describe only one request field, award_id, and the current raw profile already exposes that top-level path parameter; no additional important request-field omissions were identified.

The main MCP improvement areas are semantic rather than request-field coverage: choosing the safest downstream award identifier, branching on category-specific response families, and handling documentation mismatches such as funding_opportunity naming and total_outlay presence.
A live GET for a known contract generated award id returned HTTP 200 JSON on 2026-05-11, confirming the endpoint is currently callable.

The live contract response contained the documented common fields id, generated_unique_award_id, category, type, type_description, description, total_obligation, subaward_count, total_subaward_amount, awarding_agency, funding_agency, recipient, period_of_performance, place_of_performance, executive_details, total_account_outlay, total_account_obligation, account_outlays_by_defc, and account_obligations_by_defc.

The same live response populated procurement-specific sections including piid, latest_transaction_contract_data, parent_award, psc_hierarchy, and naics_hierarchy, confirming the contract branch behavior.

The live contract response included total_outlay with a null value, supporting the current-profile/source observation that the field is returned even though documentation is inconsistent across award families.

source.title: Reviewer report for award detail canonical award lookup alias
source.locator: Structured reviewer report selecting repair-2-canonical-award-id-alias for v2__awards__award_id

observations.statement: The reviewer-backed drilldown selected a search row with display Award ID HT940216C0001 and generated_internal_id CONT_AWD_HT940216C0001_9700_-NONE-_-NONE-, then used that generated value successfully against GET /api/v2/awards/{award_id}/.
The reviewer reported that award-detail guidance already treats generated_internal_id from spending_by_award as safer than the human-facing display Award ID for follow-up lookup.

source.title: Reviewer report for award detail canonical award lookup alias
source.locator: Structured reviewer report selecting repair-2-canonical-award-id-alias for v2__awards__award_id

observations.statement: The same generated award identifier carried through search, award detail, and award funding even though the related endpoints label that business key differently.

In the reviewer-backed workflow, v2__awards__funding accepted the search-generated hash under request field name award_id, while this award-detail endpoint documents or echoes the same business key as award_id and generated_unique_award_id.
